# Audit Readiness Record

## Current disposition

The repository can produce a hardened **assessment candidate** and an evidence bundle.
It is not operationally approved for classified processing. The mandatory open items
below cannot be completed by source code alone.

## Evidence generated by `audit.ps1`

- Security regression test results.
- Exact Git commit and tracked-tree status.
- Restricted-build profile and runtime identifier.
- Executable Authenticode status and SHA-256.
- SHA-256 manifest for every published file.
- SHA-256 manifest for every tracked source file.
- SPDX 2.3 component inventory identifying the app, self-contained .NET runtime, Ollama, and model weights.
- Copies of architecture, security, privacy, threat-model, deployment, and license documents.

Ollama and model versions are deployment-specific placeholders in the generated SPDX
document. The deployment owner must replace or supplement them with exact approved
versions, hashes, suppliers, licenses, and scan results.

## Informative control alignment

This table helps reviewers find evidence; it is not a claim of compliance.

| Reference area | Project evidence / support | Inherited or open work |
|---|---|---|
| NIST SP 800-218 SSDF: protect software, produce secure releases, respond to vulnerabilities | Git history, deterministic warning-clean build, tests, hash manifests, signing hook, disclosure process | Independent review, SAST/SCA, vulnerability operations, protected CI and signing infrastructure |
| NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-2/3/8, SA-11/15, SI-2/7, SC-7/28 | Baseline docs, change commit, tests, component inventory, integrity hashes, loopback-only design, no app persistence | Organization-selected control baseline, endpoint/network/storage controls, POA&M, assessment results |
| NIST SP 800-37 Rev. 2 / DoD RMF | Architecture, boundary, threats, deployment checklist, evidence bundle | Categorization, control selection, implementation statements, assessment, authorization, continuous monitoring |
| CNSSI 1253 for National Security Systems | Threat/boundary documentation and restricted build controls | CNSS overlay selection and assessment by qualified organizational personnel |
| NIST SP 800-171 for CUI | Local processing, release integrity, minimal data handling | CUI system assessment and organizational controls; this does not authorize classified use |

## Mandatory open items before operational promotion

- Authorizing Official decision and approved SSP/control implementation.
- Independent code/security review and documented findings disposition.
- Organization-required SAST, SCA, malware, vulnerability, and penetration testing.
- Authenticode signature and trusted timestamp from an approved signing service.
- Approved installer/distribution workflow; the current local installer is unsigned unless separately signed.
- Approved Windows baseline and STIG/security-checklist results where applicable.
- Exact approved Ollama and model inventories, hashes, signatures/provenance, licenses, and vulnerability findings.
- Verification that Ollama creates no prohibited logs, persistence, telemetry, or network traffic.
- Application allowlisting and protection against local port-11434 impersonation.
- Operational logging design that supports accountability without recording snippets or model responses.
- Classified spill, incident response, sanitization, media transfer, backup, and continuity procedures.
- Accessibility, functional, performance, and mission-owner acceptance testing.

## Official references

- [NIST SP 800-218, Secure Software Development Framework](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final)
- [NIST SP 800-37 Rev. 2, Risk Management Framework](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/37/r2/final)
- [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final)
- [NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 3, Protecting CUI](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/171/r3/final)
- [DoD Instruction 8510.01, Risk Management Framework](https://www.esd.whs.mil/Directives/issuances/dodi/)
- [CNSS Issuances, including CNSSI 1253](https://www.cnss.gov/CNSS/issuances/Instructions.cfm)

Requirements and selected controls must be confirmed against the versions adopted by
the deploying organization at assessment time.
